audit_text: Memories empty state fires on chip filter (MemoriesIndex.svelte:119-122). Spec says empty state only renders when GET /api/memories returns []. Component checks filteredRows.length === 0, so switching chip to a tag with no memories fires the onboarding copy instead of nothing. Needs fix + test.
verdict_evidence: HEAD MemoriesIndex.svelte line 119 reads `{:else if rows.length === 0}` — guard runs against the unfiltered backing array, not filteredRows. Spec is satisfied: empty-state copy renders only when the API response itself is []. `git log -- frontend/src/lib/components/memories/MemoriesIndex.svelte` returns a single commit (e814f7aa) introducing the file in its current shape; the audit-described `filteredRows.length === 0` form does not appear in any commit (confirmed via `git log --all -S 'filteredRows.length === 0'` — empty result). The audit appears to have misread the empty-state guard. The audit's secondary ask — a regression test asserting empty state stays hidden when a chip filters down to zero rows — is genuinely missing and is captured under finding-7-004.
executor_brief: No code change needed — HEAD already satisfies the spec (rows.length === 0 guard, not filteredRows). The companion regression test is tracked under finding-7-004 alongside the other test-coverage gaps.

audit_text: Memories not wired into system prompt (agent/system_prompt.py). Assembler only handles session instructions + extras — no code reads tag_memories or injects them per turn. The full CRUD surface exists but memories may never reach the model. Worker/session-bootstrap path needs audit; if genuinely missing, this is the shipped-but-dead class and the headline ship-blocker. Verify-or-fix takes priority over P0 #1.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/memories.md (whole doc; memory body is documented as 'the prompt-fragment text the assembler injects' at src/bearings/db/memories.py:58)
verdict_evidence: system_prompt.py:56-66 builds the prompt from session_instructions + extras only. options.py:426-429 calls build_system_prompt(extras=extra_system_prompt_parts). session_bootstrap.py:150 sources that tuple from `resolve_claude_md_blocks(db_connection, session_id)`. tags.py:109-140 iterates the session's tags and reads CLAUDE.md from each tag's working_dir via _load_claude_md_block (file-system read of CLAUDE.md only). Neither tags.py nor session_bootstrap.py nor any other module under src/bearings/agent/ imports memories_db; ripgrep `grep -rn 'memories_db\|tag_memories' src/bearings/agent/` returns zero substantive hits (only the misleading comment in prompt_assembler.py and import_bearings.py — neither path runs at executor-spawn time). prompt_assembler.py emits a 'tag_memory' inspector layer that itself reads CLAUDE.md from tag working_dirs (line 184-196) — not the tag_memories table — so the misnamed layer hides the gap from the Inspector view too. Net: the entire /api/memories CRUD surface (memories.py route + MemoriesEditor / MemoriesIndex frontend) writes rows the agent loop never consults. Memories never reach the model.
acceptance_criteria: A new agent-layer helper (e.g. resolve_tag_memory_blocks(connection, session_id)) reads enabled rows from the tag_memories table for every tag attached to the session, in the same precedence order as resolve_claude_md_blocks (lowest precedence first; highest last via list_for_session_ordered + reversed).
session_bootstrap.py extends the extras tuple passed to compose_session_options with the resolved tag-memory blocks so build_system_prompt emits them as system-prompt extras on every turn.

Per-turn re-read holds: editing a memory and sending the next prompt without restarting the worker reflects the new memory body in the assembled system prompt (covered by an integration-style unit test against build_system_prompt and/or the assembler).

prompt_assembler.assemble_system_prompt_layers stops conflating CLAUDE.md content with tag_memories: the 'tag_memory' kind emits one layer per ENABLED tag_memories row (with source_path=null since memories are DB-resident), and a separate kind (or repurposed PROJECT_CLAUDE_MD) carries the per-tag CLAUDE.md walk-up so the Inspector Instructions tab attributes content to its true source.
Test asserts that a session whose tag has both an enabled memory and a working-dir CLAUDE.md sees BOTH bodies in build_system_prompt output, distinguishable in the inspector layer breakdown.
Test asserts disabled (enabled=false) memories are skipped, and that deleting a memory removes its block on the next turn without runner respawn.
executor_brief: The audit's worst finding: tag_memories CRUD writes to a table the runtime never reads. Add a tag_memory loader in agent/tags.py (or a sibling memories.py under agent/) that pulls enabled tag_memories rows for the session in precedence order, and feed those bodies through extra_system_prompt_parts in session_bootstrap.py. Fix prompt_assembler.py to stop labeling CLAUDE.md content as 'tag_memory' so the Inspector reflects reality. Cover the per-turn-re-read invariant with a unit test.

audit_text: Vault cfg fallback is silent (routes/vault.py:73-87). If app.state.vault_cfg is not set, silently constructs default VaultCfg() instead of 503-ing. Inconsistent with every other subsystem (tags, DB) which fail fast. Should assert in create_app or remove fallback.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/vault.md §Configuration source (and the broader fail-fast convention shared with src/bearings/web/routes/tags.py / vault.py:_db at lines 58-70)
verdict_evidence: _cfg() at vault.py:73-87 explicitly returns `VaultCfg()` when getattr(request.app.state, 'vault_cfg', None) is None (line 81). The inline comment at lines 77-80 names this 'the production fallback', diverging from _db() at lines 58-70 in the same file which raises 503 on missing app.state attribute. Behaviour does not match the fail-fast convention the audit cites.
acceptance_criteria: _cfg() raises HTTPException(503, detail='vault_cfg not configured on app.state') when request.app.state.vault_cfg is missing or None — same shape as _db().
create_app() in src/bearings/web/app.py asserts/sets app.state.vault_cfg to a non-None VaultCfg before the router is mounted, so the production path no longer relies on _cfg's fallback.
Existing vault route tests still pass (they already inject a tmp_path-backed VaultCfg).
A new test asserts that hitting GET /api/vault when vault_cfg has been forcibly cleared from app.state returns 503 with the documented detail string.
executor_brief: Drop the `return VaultCfg()` branch from _cfg() in src/bearings/web/routes/vault.py and replace it with a 503 mirroring _db. Move the default-VaultCfg construction into create_app() so the production startup path always sets app.state.vault_cfg. Add a 503 unit test for the missing-config case.

audit_text: Frontend test coverage thin. Vault tests miss redaction toggle, search debounce, drag-paste-into-composer. Memories tests miss chip-filter-empty scenario. Write alongside fix for item 1.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/vault.md §Redaction rendering / §Search semantics / §Paste-into-message behavior; docs/behavior/memories.md §Index view layout (empty-state guard)
verdict_evidence: VaultPanel.test.ts now covers redaction toggle (lines 323-346), search debounce (lines 193-235), and paste-link / paste-body composer integration (lines 237-302) — three of the four audit-named gaps closed. Drag-paste-into-composer is the remaining gap: VaultPanel.svelte wires draggable + ondragstart at lines 370/388/428/433/453/458 calling handleDragStartVaultRow (svelte line 259-264) but no test exercises that DragEvent path. MemoriesIndex.test.ts covers the response-[] empty state (lines 46-52) and chip narrow/clear (lines 104-147) but never asserts the audit's regression invariant — that selecting a chip whose rows-after-filter is zero leaves the empty-state node hidden — so the spec at memories.md §Index view layout has no executable guard against future regressions of finding-7-001's behaviour.
acceptance_criteria: VaultPanel.test.ts gains a `describe('VaultPanel — drag-paste-into-composer')` block that fires a DragEvent on a vault row and asserts handleDragStartVaultRow called event.dataTransfer.setData (or equivalent observable side effect) with the entry's markdown_link string.

MemoriesIndex.test.ts gains a test that loads two rows for two distinct tags, clicks one chip whose tag has rows, asserts memories-index-empty is NOT rendered, then constructs a scenario (e.g. by clicking a chip whose only matching row is filtered out — using rows like [{tag_id:7,...},{tag_id:9,...}] then filtering on a tag id present in chips) and asserts the visible row count is zero AND queryByTestId('memories-index-empty') is null. The test pins the spec contract that finding-7-001's correct guard upholds.
Both tests pass under `npm test` from frontend/.
executor_brief: Three audit-named gaps were closed in-tree (redaction toggle, search debounce, paste). Two remain: a vault drag-paste DragEvent test and a memories chip-filter-zero-rows assertion that locks in the spec invariant from finding-7-001. Land the test pair without code changes.

audit_text: Vault per-doc line cap is silent. Global result cap sets SearchResult.capped flag; per-doc line cap (500 lines) quietly stops with no client signal. Inconsistent.
spec_anchor: docs/behavior/vault.md §Search semantics / §Failure modes (Search-cap reached: 'showing first N indicator is surfaced; the user is asked to narrow the query rather than the UI silently truncating with no signal')
verdict_evidence: search_vault_entries at agent/vault.py:331-366 sets `capped = True` only on the global-result-cap exits (lines 338-340 and 363-365). The per-doc bound at lines 348-350 (`if line_number > line_cap_per_doc: break`) silently exits the inner loop and continues to the next doc; SearchResult.capped is never updated. Wire shape SearchResultOut at web/models/vault.py:100-120 carries a single boolean `capped` so the frontend can't distinguish 'global cap hit' from 'this doc was truncated'. Behaviour diverges from the §Search-cap-reached contract for the per-doc case.
acceptance_criteria: agent/vault.py.search_vault_entries sets capped=True (or a distinct flag e.g. per_doc_capped, with the existing capped boolean preserved as 'global cap reached' for back-compat) when the per-doc line_cap_per_doc bound is hit on at least one doc.

SearchResultOut wire shape gains a field that lets the client distinguish global-cap from per-doc-cap (or, if a single capped flag is preferred, the spec text in vault.md is updated to read 'capped covers both global and per-doc truncation' AND the route comments / models docstring spell that out).
VaultPanel surfaces the per-doc cap to the user with copy consistent with the existing global-cap indicator.
A unit test against search_vault_entries with a doc whose body exceeds line_cap_per_doc (and whose query matches a line beyond the cap) asserts the resulting SearchResult signals truncation.
executor_brief: Add a per-doc-truncation signal to vault search. Either set capped=True on the per-doc cap path (cheapest, but loses the distinction) or extend SearchResult/SearchResultOut with a separate per_doc_capped flag and surface it in VaultPanel. Update the unit-test suite for search_vault_entries to cover the new branch.
